整套首页
unit_03 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_03

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 511 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 TC DOUBLE
Invention was (i) __________ the work of the ancient Greek historians, whose writings were filled with long and often purely fictitious speeches by great historical figures. The animating force in historical writing was rhetoric rather than (ii) __________. Even well into the eighteenth century, not a few historians continued to understand themselves as artists, given a license to invent.Invention was (i) __________ the work of the ancient Greek historians, whose writings were filled with long and often purely fictitious speeches by great historical figures. The animating force in historical writing was rhetoric rather than (ii) __________. Even well into the eighteenth century, not a few historians continued to understand themselves as artists, given a license to invent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　a hallmark of; evidence
fictitiousanimaterather
fictitious: standard unit headword | animate: standard unit headword | rather: standard unit headword
#2 TC TRIPLE
Galaxy Zoo set a standard for citizen-scientist participation project. Zealous volunteers (i)__________ the project’s organizers by classifying an entire catalog of galaxies years ahead of schedule. (ii)__________ by the (iii)__________ of the volunteer, the Galaxy Zoo team was inspired to pursue lines of research they had never even imagined.Galaxy Zoo set a standard for citizen-scientist participation project. Zealous volunteers (i)__________ the project’s organizers by classifying an entire catalog of galaxies years ahead of schedule. (ii)__________ by the (iii)__________ of the volunteer, the Galaxy Zoo team was inspired to pursue lines of research they had never even imagined.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　astonished; Buoyed; alacrity
buoyzealousbaffle
buoy: standard unit headword | zealous: standard unit headword | baffle: standard unit headword
#3 TC DOUBLE
Mr. Stevens found that home schooling, far from representing (i)__________ philosophy, (ii)__________ some of the most widely accepted education ideas that children should be treated as individuals, taught in small numbers, and even given a measure of discretion over their own learning.Mr. Stevens found that home schooling, far from representing (i)__________ philosophy, (ii)__________ some of the most widely accepted education ideas that children should be treated as individuals, taught in small numbers, and even given a measure of discretion over their own learning.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　an anomalous; embodies
philosophyrepresentanticipate
philosophy: standard unit headword | represent: standard unit headword | anticipate: standard unit headword
#4 SE
As a way of __________ the negative impacts of overdependence on a single export product—crude oil—the Nigerian government passed legislation in 1999 intended to revitalize the moribund solid minerals sector.As a way of __________ the negative impacts of overdependence on a single export product—crude oil—the Nigerian government passed legislation in 1999 intended to revitalize the moribund solid minerals sector.
答案：D, E　checking; stemming
cruderevitalize
crude: standard unit headword | revitalize: standard unit headword
#5 TC SINGLE
Other company insiders have recently offered testimony that __________ several of the more lurid anecdotes found in Belmer's account, though that testimony hardly minimizes the company's culture of malfeasance and mismanagement.Other company insiders have recently offered testimony that __________ several of the more lurid anecdotes found in Belmer's account, though that testimony hardly minimizes the company's culture of malfeasance and mismanagement.
答案：A　tempers
testimonyaccount
testimony: standard unit headword | account: standard unit headword
#6 TC TRIPLE
Oral traditions are (i) __________ nearly everywhere, and in nonliterate societies they do not (ii) __________ at their first exposure to the written word. Despite Goody's contention that a kind of literacy line cuts through history, dividing oral from print cultures, it seems that traditional tale-telling can (iii) __________ long after a print culture is established.Oral traditions are (i) __________ nearly everywhere, and in nonliterate societies they do not (ii) __________ at their first exposure to the written word. Despite Goody's contention that a kind of literacy line cuts through history, dividing oral from print cultures, it seems that traditional tale-telling can (iii) __________ long after a print culture is established.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　tenacious; collapse; flourish
languishtenaciousdivide
languish: standard unit headword | tenacious: standard unit headword | divide: standard unit headword
#7 SE
British critics covering African American musicians performing in London in the 1910s had little idea how to distinguish what was authentic African American music from what was __________, but they knew such a distinction existed.British critics covering African American musicians performing in London in the 1910s had little idea how to distinguish what was authentic African American music from what was __________, but they knew such a distinction existed.
答案：D, F　spurious; specious
authenticcover
authentic: standard unit headword | cover: standard unit headword
#8 TC SINGLE
Parker's model of human reflects a __________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally pessimistic analyses of her colleagues in the economics department.Parker's model of human reflects a __________ outlook, in stark contrast to the generally pessimistic analyses of her colleagues in the economics department.
答案：E　sanguine
reflectprescient
reflect: standard unit headword | prescient: standard unit headword
#9 TC DOUBLE
The company's chief executive is (i) __________ the desire to postpone action until a consensus has been reached. Instead she makes a brief for instinctive decision making, treating extensive deliberation as something to (ii) __________.The company's chief executive is (i) __________ the desire to postpone action until a consensus has been reached. Instead she makes a brief for instinctive decision making, treating extensive deliberation as something to (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　skeptical of; shun
implementshunpostpone
implement: standard unit headword | shun: standard unit headword | postpone: standard unit headword
#10 TC TRIPLE
In adolescence, (i)__________ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)__________ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)__________ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.In adolescence, (i)__________ interactions are crucial in forging a self-identity. To be sure, this process often plays out in (ii)__________ as a means of defining and shoring up the sense of self. Kids will seek out like-minded companions, and spurn others who seem different. But when kept within reasonable bounds, this in-group (iii)__________ generally evolves into a more mature friendship pattern.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　peer; cliquish social behavior; clustering
shore upbound
shore up: standard unit headword | bound: standard unit headword
#11 SE
Culture, like speech, is primarily a human faculty, although both functions may exist in a more __________ form in lesser primates.Culture, like speech, is primarily a human faculty, although both functions may exist in a more __________ form in lesser primates.
答案：C, F　primitive; rudimentary
lesserindispensable
lesser: standard unit headword | indispensable: standard unit headword
#12 TC SINGLE
Although most of the lakes have merged, salinity levels are not __________ throughout the lake system: an upward-trending salinity gradient extends southeast from Pelican Lake to East Stump Lake, a distance of nearly 80 kilometers.Although most of the lakes have merged, salinity levels are not __________ throughout the lake system: an upward-trending salinity gradient extends southeast from Pelican Lake to East Stump Lake, a distance of nearly 80 kilometers.
答案：B　equable
stumpequable
stump: standard unit headword | equable: standard unit headword
#13 TC TRIPLE
One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.One sometimes hears that Marco Polo introduced pasta to the western world, having encountered it in China. This durable myth, which (i)__________ that nothing should have been known of pasta in Italy until 1295, when Marco Polo returned from the Far East, can easily be (ii)__________ by pointing out that there are Italian references to pasta that (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　requires; debunked; predated that event
augmentauthenticate
augment: standard unit headword | authenticate: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　emulate; anachronistic
cherishrathercommend

#15 SE
The composer’s string quartets exhibit a structural __________ that seems to challenge the conventions of the string quartet genre, which have long been celebrated for their rigor and coherence.The composer’s string quartets exhibit a structural __________ that seems to challenge the conventions of the string quartet genre, which have long been celebrated for their rigor and coherence.
答案：C, F　uncertainty; instability
rigorvitality
rigor: standard unit headword | vitality: standard unit headword

There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)__________. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.Pioneering medical research scientists’ (i)__________ claims regarding the (ii)__________ new researches make the public wonder why their own doctors are not dispensing miracles. There are forces, both external and internal, on scientists that most require them to (iii)__________. Without money, there is no science. Researchers must constantly convince administrators who control tax dollars, investors, and individual donors that the work they are doing will make a difference.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　inflated; potential benefits of; oversell
dispenseinflate
dispense: standard unit headword | inflate: standard unit headword

Specifically, the pulsar's periodic radiation pattern was initially interpreted as (iii) __________ rather than--as it turned out to be--the natural product of the spinning of a neutron star.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　thought that; suggestive of; a constructed code
hoaxepisodicrather
hoax: standard unit headword | episodic: standard unit headword | rather: standard unit headword

答案：B　list
posture

Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　characterized; lack; dearth

答案：B, E　contemplative; ruminative
ponderrather

Many were uncomfortable at the way the corporation was forced to spend in much of last year’s expansion, and this new venture is potentially even more risky.

Blank 2

Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.Cynics will dismiss the race between Richard and Gorman as two equally dull candidates. However, the notion that the two leaders are (i)__________ does not meet them, for they are as different as can be. Richard is (ii)__________ and is fastidious of her appearance. So one could hardly accuse her of being either taciturn or (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　interchangeable; loquacious; unkempt
interchangeableblunt
interchangeable: standard unit headword | blunt: standard unit headword

答案：B　esoteric
compelling

答案：A, F　asserts; maintains

#25 TC DOUBLE

But village cinemas quickly became (ii)__________ even though they were ramshackle affairs in comparison to the urban picture palaces.Until the advent of film, commercial entertainment in England occurred only where concentrated urban populations provided audiences large enough to make it remunerative; theaters and music halls were (i)__________ in rural villages. But village cinemas quickly became (ii)__________ even though they were ramshackle affairs in comparison to the urban picture palaces.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　inconceivable; commonplace
ramshackle
ramshackle: standard unit headword

She constantly __________ herself for not living up to her own ideals—for not working hard enough or not having motives that were pure enough.She constantly __________ herself for not living up to her own ideals—for not working hard enough or not having motives that were pure enough.
答案：C　excoriated
excoriate
excoriate: standard unit headword
#27 SE
While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such __________ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.While recognizing that recent reports of cyberwarfare, phone-hacking scandals, and identity thefts have tended to accent the destructive connotation of the word, Sue Halpern maintains that “hacking” is such __________ term that its meaning nearly always derives from its context.
答案：A, B　a generic; an inclusive
accent
accent: standard unit headword

It retains this potency partly because its control of the nation's banking system is (i) __________ governmental interference, and thus its actions remain largely (ii) __________.The national bank has been uncommonly powerful in comparison to its counterparts in other nations. It retains this potency partly because its control of the nation's banking system is (i) __________ governmental interference, and thus its actions remain largely (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　unencumbered by; discretionary
potency
potency: standard unit headword
#29 SE
In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Peggy Guggenheim was __________: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.In matters of taste, the art patron and collector Peggy Guggenheim was __________: she was for the strangest, the most surprising, the most satisfying, the best, the unique.
答案：D, F　an extremist; a zealot
zealot
zealot: standard unit headword

答案：E　exculpation
ministration
ministration: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　smothering; unquestioningly embrace
squelch

答案：B, F　a hierarchical; a stratified
stratify

答案：A　significance

Wolosky's description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.Wolosky claims that Ella Wheeler Wilcox joined other women poets such as Julia Ward Howe, Frances Harper, and Charlotte Perkins Gilman in critiquing materialism and possessive individualism. Wolosky's description (i)__________ the poetry of Gilman, Howe, and Harper, but it is not entirely (ii)__________ in the case of Wilcox, who hardly (iii)__________ the materialism of her time. Rather, Wilcox seems to have embraced the amassing of private property.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　fits; accurate; impugned

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　malady; healthful; funds

While the professor was once known for expressing herself (i) __________, that is no longer true. Her writing style is much more (ii) __________ than it was when she started her career over 30 years ago.While the professor was once known for expressing herself (i) __________, that is no longer true. Her writing style is much more (ii) __________ than it was when she started her career over 30 years ago.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　plainly; florid
acerbic
acerbic: standard unit headword
#52 SE
Each of the country’s 26 states and most of its more than 2,000 municipalities have their own police forces, __________ communication and making it difficult to establish a coordinated law-enforcement strategy.Each of the country’s 26 states and most of its more than 2,000 municipalities have their own police forces, __________ communication and making it difficult to establish a coordinated law-enforcement strategy.
答案：B, C　impeding; hampering
hamper
hamper: standard unit headword
#53 TC SINGLE
In the 1980s the press release announcing the discovery of a new fundamental particle turned a once __________ hypothesis of interest to no more than a few hundred physicists into an undisputed fact of global significance.In the 1980s the press release announcing the discovery of a new fundamental particle turned a once __________ hypothesis of interest to no more than a few hundred physicists into an undisputed fact of global significance.
答案：C　recondite
cogent
cogent: standard unit headword
#54 TC TRIPLE
Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey's book is representative of a heightened sense of (i) __________. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii) __________ the (iii) __________ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.Most scientific biographies put their subjects on pedestals, but times are changing, and Coffey's book is representative of a heightened sense of (i) __________. Weaving together the lives of the leaders of modern chemistry, Coffey shows how fights over priority, backstabbing, cronyism, and grudges shaped the history of chemistry just as much as the actual discoveries did. It is an effective (ii) __________ the (iii) __________ that science is the work of utterly selfless researchers.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　candor; antidote to; banal notion
cronyism
cronyism: standard unit headword
#55 TC DOUBLE
The Newseum is a museum created by, for, and about journalists and newshounds. But for all its celebration of the news industry, its creators have taken care to avoid (i) __________. Along with the many testimonials to journalistic courage and a memorial to journalists who lost their lives on the job, the museum presents noteworthy examples of journalistic (ii) __________.The Newseum is a museum created by, for, and about journalists and newshounds. But for all its celebration of the news industry, its creators have taken care to avoid (i) __________. Along with the many testimonials to journalistic courage and a memorial to journalists who lost their lives on the job, the museum presents noteworthy examples of journalistic (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　puffery; malfeasance
virtuosity
virtuosity: standard unit headword
#56 SE
The baseball players’ performance during last night’s game was impressive, although not __________: others have performed similar feats.The baseball players’ performance during last night’s game was impressive, although not __________: others have performed similar feats.
答案：B, E　unexampled; outstanding
unexampled
unexampled: standard unit headword
#57 TC TRIPLE
In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.In framing old paintings, after the gilding comes a process that is freer and more like art: toning the gold and distressing it, so that the frame does not look new. Since verisimilitude is sought, the aim in adding apparent age to the new surface must be to (i) __________, circumventing the human eye's adeptness at spotting anything that seems (ii) __________. Different framers have different ways of (iii) __________ the effect of chance.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, I　conceal the artifice; intentional; mimicking
distressing
distressing: standard unit headword
#58 TC DOUBLE
The description of humans as having an internal clock is not a (i) __________. Or rather, it is—you do not have a tiny watch in your cerebellum—but it also refers to (ii) __________, a specialized bundle of cells that regulates cyclical processes.The description of humans as having an internal clock is not a (i) __________. Or rather, it is—you do not have a tiny watch in your cerebellum—but it also refers to (ii) __________, a specialized bundle of cells that regulates cyclical processes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　metaphor; a real biological feature

Very few companies take the trouble to discover where the wood in their products originates, consumers do not demand this information, and consequently __________ regarding illicit timber has become the norm.Very few companies take the trouble to discover where the wood in their products originates, consumers do not demand this information, and consequently __________ regarding illicit timber has become the norm.
答案：B, F　indifference; apathy
apathy
apathy: standard unit headword
#60 TC TRIPLE
The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and, to the extent practicable and permitted by law, since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i)__________ their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii)__________ measures to (iii)__________ risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.The order applies to all Federal agency whose actions may affect the status of invasive species and requires agencies to identify such actions and, to the extent practicable and permitted by law, since invasive species severely reduce the number of native species and even (i)__________ their existence, the agency has determined and made public its determination that the benefits of such actions clearly outweigh the potential harm caused by invasive species; and that all feasible and (ii)__________ measures to (iii)__________ risk of harm of the introduction of invasive species will be taken in conjunction with the actions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　preclude; prudent; minimize
counterbalance
counterbalance: standard unit headword
#61 SE
For wily political press officers, the art of spin is not quite supplanting truth with lies, but instead it aspires to replace awkward complexity with catchy __________; successful spin creates the impression of unavoidable common sense.For wily political press officers, the art of spin is not quite supplanting truth with lies, but instead it aspires to replace awkward complexity with catchy __________; successful spin creates the impression of unavoidable common sense.
答案：E, F　simplicity; intelligibility

答案：A, B　presage; foretell

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, H　retain; condemnation of; forgo

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　unsurprising; minimal; obscured by

答案：A, C　wayward; errant

If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build a population from scratch.If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition since the plan would then involve __________ a historic population, not trying to build a population from scratch.
答案：B　augmenting

答案：B, D　unlinked; estranged

Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)__________ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)__________ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)__________ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.Laws protecting intellectual property are intended to stimulate creativity, yet some forms of creative work have never enjoyed legal protection—a situation that ought to be of great interest. If we see certain forms of creative endeavor (i)__________ as a result of uncontrolled copying, we might decide to (ii)__________ intellectual property law. Conversely, if unprotected creative work (iii)__________ in the absence of legal rules against copying, we would do well to know how such flourishing is sustained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　languishing; extend; manages to thrive

答案：D　immutable

Their occurrence implies conditions on the bog surface suitable for colonization, followed by inhospitable conditions for trees that nevertheless facilitated preservation of stumps. Thus, the (i)__________ of pine stumps can (ii)__________ climate change, by (iii)__________ the inference that bog surfaces had dried sufficiently to allow colonization and then became too wet to support the trees.Pine stumps have been found in discrete layers in peat deposits throughout western Europe and thus represent brief but distinct episodes of bog colonization by pine. Their occurrence implies conditions on the bog surface suitable for colonization, followed by inhospitable conditions for trees that nevertheless facilitated preservation of stumps. Thus, the (i)__________ of pine stumps can (ii)__________ climate change, by (iii)__________ the inference that bog surfaces had dried sufficiently to allow colonization and then became too wet to support the trees.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　presence; function as a proxy for; supporting

In a view of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.In a view of 17 studies from 2008, Trudeau and Shephard concluded that reserving up to an hour a day for (i)__________ in school curricula does not (ii)__________ academic achievement. In fact, they noted that more exercise often (iii)__________ school performance, despite the time it took away from reading, writing, and arithmetic.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, H　physical activity; detract from; augmented
augment
augment: standard unit headword
#103 TC DOUBLE
Any account of experimental music in the United States that (i)__________ the predominantly African American bebop and free jazz movements is (ii)__________, since this body of music constitutes what is arguably the most influential African experimental music in the decades following the Second World War.Any account of experimental music in the United States that (i)__________ the predominantly African American bebop and free jazz movements is (ii)__________, since this body of music constitutes what is arguably the most influential African experimental music in the decades following the Second World War.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　neglects; problematic
account
account: standard unit headword
#104 TC SINGLE
By deliberately dripping paint on their canvases, expressionists affirmed that paint is not __________ entity, something merely to be moved where the artist pleases, but rather a material possessing a fluid energy that the painter attempts to control.By deliberately dripping paint on their canvases, expressionists affirmed that paint is not __________ entity, something merely to be moved where the artist pleases, but rather a material possessing a fluid energy that the painter attempts to control.
答案：D　a passive
rather
rather: standard unit headword
#105 SE
Despite her rather __________ choices, Moreland was neither a rebellious spirit nor someone who saw herself as anything out of the ordinary.Despite her rather __________ choices, Moreland was neither a rebellious spirit nor someone who saw herself as anything out of the ordinary.
答案：A, C　unconventional; quirky
rather
rather: standard unit headword

The "or else" included a long list of (ii)__________ that (iii)__________ the developed world, from heart disease and osteoporosis to diabetes.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　make public; maladies; plague

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　palliative; curative
rather
rather: standard unit headword
#108 TC SINGLE
According to the ancient Chinese philosopher Mozi, the world's problems stem from __________: rather than desiring what is best for the community, people care most about themselves, then their family and friends, and little about others.According to the ancient Chinese philosopher Mozi, the world's problems stem from __________: rather than desiring what is best for the community, people care most about themselves, then their family and friends, and little about others.
答案：E　partiality
rather
rather: standard unit headword
#109 SE
Even the cleverest use of time management techniques is powerless to __________ sum of minutes in a person’s life, so people squeeze as much as they could into each one of them.Even the cleverest use of time management techniques is powerless to __________ sum of minutes in a person’s life, so people squeeze as much as they could into each one of them.
答案：C, F　augment; extend
augment
augment: standard unit headword
#110 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as “social atoms” that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another—in an effort to avoid collisions—and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　law-like regularities; chaotic; self-perpetuating
rather
rather: standard unit headword
#111 TC DOUBLE
Though McDonough (i)__________ discusses the filmmaker’s aesthetic principles, it is the description of the (ii)__________, the very vulgarity of the director’s films, rather than McDonough’s learned discourses on the aesthetics of film, that makes the book so entertaining.Though McDonough (i)__________ discusses the filmmaker’s aesthetic principles, it is the description of the (ii)__________, the very vulgarity of the director’s films, rather than McDonough’s learned discourses on the aesthetics of film, that makes the book so entertaining.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　adroitly; flagrant crassness

答案：C　excoriated
excoriate

They can, however, make it more (iii)__________ to communicate scientific developments, even to colleague.While some commentators suggest that abstraction and complexity in scientific research are signs that a given direction is misguided, Lisa Randall, a professor of physics at Harvard, counters that these qualities instead reflect the success of human ingenuity in (i)__________ the increasingly (ii)__________ challenges that nature presents. Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　meeting; difficult; challenging

答案：B, E　bedrock; foundation

Blank 2
答案：B, D　sobering; entreating

答案：A　zealous

#117 TC TRIPLE
How does one evaluate the effects of modern democracy on individuals? The first clarifying step must be to recognize that “democracy” itself can, in the abstract, (i)__________ us as we think of our society and our perception of democracy as citizens. The experience of living in a democracy (ii)__________ each generation. A feature of democratic is that something that benefits us in one generation may no longer be a benefit to the next. Thus, experiencing democracy in the twenty-first-century is (iii)__________ the political order our ancestors cherished in 1901.How does one evaluate the effects of modern democracy on individuals? The first clarifying step must be to recognize that “democracy” itself can, in the abstract, (i)__________ us as we think of our society and our perception of democracy as citizens. The experience of living in a democracy (ii)__________ each generation. A feature of democratic is that something that benefits us in one generation may no longer be a benefit to the next. Thus, experiencing democracy in the twenty-first-century is (iii)__________ the political order our ancestors cherished in 1901.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　mislead; changes for; radically different from

答案：B, F　riddle; conundrum
stump

Blank 2
答案：C, E　divided; halfhearted

答案：B　impede the growth of

One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)__________, exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes. The process is made easier when what we are learning (ii)__________ our built-in circuitry. With an understanding of it, we can at least (iii)__________ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while the number sense may be (i)__________, exact calculation requires cultural tools—symbols and algorithms —that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes. The process is made easier when what we are learning (ii)__________ our built-in circuitry. With an understanding of it, we can at least (iii)__________ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　innate; harmonizes with; adapt
reflect
reflect: standard unit headword

答案：E　disappearance

Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized

But village cinemas quickly became (ii) __________, even though they were ramshackle affairs in comparison to the urban picture palaces.Until the advent of film, commercial entertainment in England occurred only where concentrated urban populations provided audiences large enough to make it remunerative: theaters and music halls were (i) __________ in rural villages. But village cinemas quickly became (ii) __________, even though they were ramshackle affairs in comparison to the urban picture palaces.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　unconceivable; commonplace

答案：D　indispensable

The fiction writer in him, however, was hard to (ii)__________. Handi Murdd is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale’s creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy’s nonfiction.Leo Tolstoy wrote many works of nonfiction and professed (i)__________ these explorations of ethics and religion compared with his novels and short stories. Handi Murdd is a short novel with the breadth and power of an epic, with vivid characterization and intense storytelling that sweep the reader away. While the reader senses the moral concerns of the tale’s creator, the novel is a far cry from the (iii)__________ of Tolstoy’s nonfiction.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　a preference for; suppress; didacticism

The controversy about Alexander the Great's personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i) __________, all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii) __________ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander's alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.The controversy about Alexander the Great's personality derives from the fact that our sources are (i) __________, all eyewitness accounts having perished. What remains is, at best, (ii) __________ (one history, for instance, is based largely on the now-lost memoirs of Alexander's alleged half-brother, Ptolemy) and at worst, highly unreliable.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　inadequate; secondhand

答案：A　crude

#153 SE

答案：B, C　immaterial; inconsequential

Blank 2

If newspaper consumers are concerned about more than (i) __________ and prefer to read news that is consistent with their beliefs, then (ii) __________ is not a journalistic flaw, but, rather, a cultivated feature. In a competitive news market, producers can use slant to differentiate their products and stave off price competition.If newspaper consumers are concerned about more than (i) __________ and prefer to read news that is consistent with their beliefs, then (ii) __________ is not a journalistic flaw, but, rather, a cultivated feature. In a competitive news market, producers can use slant to differentiate their products and stave off price competition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　accuracy; bias
rather
rather: standard unit headword
#156 TC SINGLE
Although most of the lakes have merged, salinity levels are not __________ throughout the lake systems: an upward-trending salinity gradient extends southeast from Pelican Lake to East Stump Lake, a distance of nearly 80 kilometers.Although most of the lakes have merged, salinity levels are not __________ throughout the lake systems: an upward-trending salinity gradient extends southeast from Pelican Lake to East Stump Lake, a distance of nearly 80 kilometers.
答案：B　equable
stumpequable
stump: standard unit headword | equable: standard unit headword
#157 SE
Church, Nussbaum, Waldrnan, and Wills have written very different books—Nussbaurm and Wills range both farther and deeper—but each one of the four strives for __________, wanting to save us from the errors of partisans and zealots.Church, Nussbaum, Waldrnan, and Wills have written very different books—Nussbaurm and Wills range both farther and deeper—but each one of the four strives for __________, wanting to save us from the errors of partisans and zealots.
答案：B, F　evenhandedness; impartiality
zealot
zealot: standard unit headword
#158 TC TRIPLE
Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.Both very good and very bad books are easy to review. Praise and (i)__________ come easily. But what of books that contain a muddle of virtues and vices? Here the reviewer's task is more (ii)__________: the author's useful and thought-provoking observations need to be (iii)__________ the useless and tedious.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　censure; demanding; sifted from
thought-provoking
thought-provoking: standard unit headword
#159 TC DOUBLE
Unlike the elected branches of the United States government, where making personal connection with citizens is (i) __________ and almost (ii) __________ political efficacy, the United States Supreme Court continues to maintain that its members should communicate with the public almost exclusively through formal opinions—and even then through ceremonial rituals that date back to the nineteenth century.Unlike the elected branches of the United States government, where making personal connection with citizens is (i) __________ and almost (ii) __________ political efficacy, the United States Supreme Court continues to maintain that its members should communicate with the public almost exclusively through formal opinions—and even then through ceremonial rituals that date back to the nineteenth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　rampant; a requirement for
rampant
rampant: standard unit headword
#160 TC SINGLE
Research indicates that the habitat __________ commonly observed for meadow voles and prairie voles living in a single ecosystem results primarily from species differences in habitat tolerance, with interspecific competition playing a lesser role.Research indicates that the habitat __________ commonly observed for meadow voles and prairie voles living in a single ecosystem results primarily from species differences in habitat tolerance, with interspecific competition playing a lesser role.
答案：E　segregation
lesser
lesser: standard unit headword
#161 SE
As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her __________: rather than presenting fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.As a critic, Nelson is noteworthy for her __________: rather than presenting fully formed pronouncements, she is willing to let us watch as she works out her ideas.
答案：B, D　humility; modesty
rather
rather: standard unit headword
#162 TC TRIPLE
Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)__________ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)__________ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)__________ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.Campus-wide discussion on academic integrity can be (i)__________ by the fact that faculty and students tend to define cheating in (ii)__________ ways. Even when they concur on what cheating means, faculty and students often assign different levels of severity to specific violations. These differences can serve as a major (iii)__________ the creation of a commonly accepted set of standards of integrity that are consistently applied to all academic work within the campus community.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　hampered; disparate; impediment to
hamper
hamper: standard unit headword
#163 TC DOUBLE
The mood of the times is no longer one of (i) __________ over our scientific achievements. Doubts and worries beset technical and scientific specialists, as well as the public at large. I do not consider such worries (ii) __________, though they are often based on intuitive feeling rather than on strictly logical arguments.The mood of the times is no longer one of (i) __________ over our scientific achievements. Doubts and worries beset technical and scientific specialists, as well as the public at large. I do not consider such worries (ii) __________, though they are often based on intuitive feeling rather than on strictly logical arguments.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　uncritical joy; unfounded

#164 TC SINGLE
Science is a seemingly inexhaustible source of __________, yet paradoxically scientists have been portrayed in recent decades as rather conservative--resisting innovation in their discipline sometimes to the point of dogmatism.Science is a seemingly inexhaustible source of __________, yet paradoxically scientists have been portrayed in recent decades as rather conservative--resisting innovation in their discipline sometimes to the point of dogmatism.
答案：C　novelty

rather: standard unit headword
#165 TC TRIPLE
Given our species' increasing numbers and appetites--which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change--even (i)__________ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)__________, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)__________.Given our species' increasing numbers and appetites--which are reflected in and compounded by global climate change--even (i)__________ species are likely to become endangered within the foreseeable future. If we are to avoid a calamitous loss of biodiversity, according to Golbe, we cannot simply let nature take its course. Ongoing conservation management is (ii)__________, as we have, in a sense, become nature, and the responsibilities that come with the role cannot be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　now common; obligatory; jettisoned

答案：B, C　proclaimed; professed

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　admirable; unusual

答案：A　parsimony
dispense

The performer can be __________ in his comedy, but he is fundamentally a bighearted person who displays a core sweetness even at his most manic states.The performer can be __________ in his comedy, but he is fundamentally a bighearted person who displays a core sweetness even at his most manic states.
答案：A, F　inflammatory; provocative

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　questionable; inherently implausible about; conjecture

We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.Nordhaus predicts that in the future we will increasingly be (i) __________ ecological problems like global warming rather than (ii) __________ them. We may, for example, make some headway in limiting emissions that contribute to warming, but much of our work will be in adapting to ecological problems and alleviating their effects.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　managing; solving

Barbara Johnson's 1982 essay on Mary Shelley's Frankenstein drew upon feminist texts written for a popular, rather than academic, audience without __________: Johnson's critique is dense and theoretically sophisticated.Barbara Johnson's 1982 essay on Mary Shelley's Frankenstein drew upon feminist texts written for a popular, rather than academic, audience without __________: Johnson's critique is dense and theoretically sophisticated.
答案：B　sacrificing rigor

The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.The slow pace of job creation was without precedent for the period of recovery from a recession, but the conditions that conspired to cause the recession were also (i)__________. The stock market declined sharply, and rampant business investment slumped. Then an ensuing spate of scandals (ii)__________ public trust in the way companies were run. And yet, despite these powerful (iii)__________ to growth, the recession proved surprisingly mild.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　atypical; weakened; counterforces
rampant
rampant: standard unit headword

It can be (i)__________ to read Margaret Fuller's travel writing, as she produced accounts of her travel that (ii)__________ conventions of bourgeois travel narrative, often capitulating to the most well-worn cliches of the genre at precisely the moments when she sought most energetically to cast them off in favor of some new, more passionate mode of discernment.It can be (i)__________ to read Margaret Fuller's travel writing, as she produced accounts of her travel that (ii)__________ conventions of bourgeois travel narrative, often capitulating to the most well-worn cliches of the genre at precisely the moments when she sought most energetically to cast them off in favor of some new, more passionate mode of discernment.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　frustrating; conformed to

One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely __________ fossils throughout his career.One of the greatest hoaxes in the history of science was perpetrated by the Indian paleontologist Dr. Chandra Gupta, who fabricated entirely __________ fossils throughout his career.
答案：A, F　counterfeit; spurious

答案：E, F　essential for; indispensable to

#178 TC TRIPLE
Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.Viewing people as "social atoms" that obey rather simple rule (which are not unlike the laws of physics), one may discover certain (i)__________. Take, for example, the way channels emerge when people move in crowds. In the midst of initially (ii)__________ movements, one person begins to follow another--in an effort to avoid collisions--and streams of movement emerge. As more people join in, there is greater pull on others to join the flow, and the particular channels become (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　lawlike regulations; chaotic; self-perpetuating
rather
rather: standard unit headword

Blank 2
答案：B, E　does not reflect; rare

答案：D　uncomfortable precedent

They can, however, make it more (iii)__________ to communicate scientific developments, even to colleagues.While some commentators suggest that abstraction and complexity in scientific research are signs that a given direction is misguided, Lisa Randall, a professor of physics at Harvard, counters that these qualities instead reflect the success of human ingenuity in (i)__________ the increasingly (ii)__________ challenges that nature presents. Blank 2

答案：B, D, G　meeting; difficult; challenging

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　unimpressed with; terrifying; a commonplace

At first such observations tend to be treated as (i) __________, but those scientists (ii) __________ them sometimes discover that they have to abandon established principles in order to account for the new findings.

答案：E　superior to

However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book's value had he more effectively (ii)__________ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)__________ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy; understandably, space in such a book is (i)__________, and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book's value had he more effectively (ii)__________ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)__________ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　at a premium; signaled; sense of

Blank 2

So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)__________ if only the public schools did a better job.Movement from bottom to top, from poor to rich, was rare; even movement from poor to middle class was (i)__________. Statistical analysis of trends in occupation, income, and property ownership, Thernstrom wrote, "yielded rather (ii)__________ conclusions about social mobility in nineteenth-century America." So we might expect Thernstrom to be suspicious now of claims that differences in class could be (iii)__________ if only the public schools did a better job.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　an anomaly; pessimistic; obliterated

答案：B, C　prescient; prophetic

Blank 2

答案：A　impracticable
prescient
prescient: standard unit headword
#205 TC TRIPLE
Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also a misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons is that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also a misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons is that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)__________, communal devil feeding is (ii)__________ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)__________ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized
posture
posture: standard unit headword
#206 SE
Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can __________ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.Some have argued that naming scientific discoveries after the people who make them can __________ scientific progress, because the nomenclature lacks useful clarity, having no relationship to the underlying principles that govern nature.
答案：A, F　hamper; impede
hamper
hamper: standard unit headword

Federal efforts to regulate standards on educational achievements have been met by (i) __________ from the states; local governments feel that government imposition represents an undue infringement on their (ii) __________.Federal efforts to regulate standards on educational achievements have been met by (i) __________ from the states; local governments feel that government imposition represents an undue infringement on their (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　intransigence; autonomy

答案：C　enigmatic

zealous: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　palliative; curative

These climatologists say the evidence of a tie between carbon dioxide and planetary warming over the last few centuries is so (ii)__________ that any longer-term evidence against such a link must somehow be (iii)__________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　irrelevant; compelling; tainted

答案：C　lucidity
rigor

#218 TC DOUBLE
To the avid reader of E.O. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) __________, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii) __________ and ambition.To the avid reader of E.O. Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i) __________, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predictable; purview

答案：D, E　mordant; acerbic

People who are normally (ii)__________ may, when describing their commutes, be unexpectedly (iii)__________ divulging the intimate details of their lives.People love to talk about their commutes to and from work: those with an easy commute tend to (i)__________, while those who hate their commute think and speak of it as a core affliction, like a chronic illness. Newman and Principe suggest that a third term, "chymistry," should be used to denote the transitional early modern phase when "alchemy" and "chemistry" were becoming (iii)__________.William Newman and Lawrence Principe point out that prior to the seventeenth century the terms "alchemy" and "chemistry" were largely (i)__________ in European usage. It was from the third decade of the eighteenth century that the two terms began to (ii)__________. Blank 1

K. Saho notes that it is difficult to fully (i) __________ many of the oral accounts on which the study relies. It seems odd to imagine a duck paddling alongside a Spinosaurus, but the molecular evidence for the (i) __________ of modern birds and dinosaurs is so (ii) __________ that even paleontologists--who have typically viewed with skepticism those DNA findings that conflict with the fossil record--have begun to (iii) __________ it.Certain molecular studies imply that, contrary to conventional scientific wisdom, neornithines (modern birds) lived alongside dinosaurs instead of having evolved after dinosaurs' extinction. It seems odd to imagine a duck paddling alongside a Spinosaurus, but the molecular evidence for the (i) __________ of modern birds and dinosaurs is so (ii) __________ that even paleontologists--who have typically viewed with skepticism those DNA findings that conflict with the fossil record--have begun to (iii) __________ it.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　contemporaneity; compelling; embrace

With an understanding of it we can at least (iii)__________ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.One of the fundamental problems with learning mathematics is that while number sense may be (i)__________, exact calculation requires cultural tools - symbols and algorithms - that are relatively new and must therefore be absorbed by areas of the brain designed for other purposes, which is easier when what we are learning (ii)__________ our built-in circuitry. With an understanding of it we can at least (iii)__________ our teaching methods by reflecting on the constraints it imposes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　innate; harmonizes with; adapt

Yet the tendency to judge the cosmos based on appearances and assumptions of our own (i) __________ has proved (ii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2

This conclusion would be (ii)__________, however, insofar as not all nostalgia necessarily results in a (iii)__________ of critical thought.Logically, one might assume that the nostalgia in which Guzman engages at times in his documentary about Chilean history would be (i)__________ critical reflection and thus leave the film intellectually flaccid. Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, I　an impediment to; hasty; stagnation

nostalgia: standard unit headword
#259 SE
A major obstacle to reliably predicting the effects of changed hydrology on riverine biodiversity is the __________ of information on certain river fishes' breeding habits and seasonal dynamics: basic ecosystem data are almost entirely lacking.A major obstacle to reliably predicting the effects of changed hydrology on riverine biodiversity is the __________ of information on certain river fishes' breeding habits and seasonal dynamics: basic ecosystem data are almost entirely lacking.
答案：A, D　Dearth; paucity
dearth
dearth: standard unit headword
#260 TC SINGLE
In 1831, a domestic insurrection that had stirred central Italy was squelched by the Austrian army, but while this invasion destroyed the immediate results of the movement, it could not __________ the insurrections causes, which continued to be felt.In 1831, a domestic insurrection that had stirred central Italy was squelched by the Austrian army, but while this invasion destroyed the immediate results of the movement, it could not __________ the insurrections causes, which continued to be felt.
答案：B　extirpate
squelchanticipate
squelch: standard unit headword | anticipate: standard unit headword
#261 TC TRIPLE
The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)__________ is the cowbirds' habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)__________ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)__________, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.The cowbird can seem a rather comical creature with a slow, awkward walk and often upraised tail. Less (i)__________ is the cowbirds' habit of laying their eggs in the nests of other birds. The (ii)__________ nesters will usually accept the cowbird egg and raise the baby cowbird as their own. Unfortunately, cowbird eggs hatch sooner than the eggs of other species and the young cowbirds (iii)__________, using their size to their advantage in getting more food from the parents.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, G　amusing; unwitting; grow quickly
rather
rather: standard unit headword
#262 TC DOUBLE
The dazzling extent and variety of Pearigen's work (i) __________ those who search for artistic continuity; nonetheless, certain ingrained values are (ii) __________ throughout his oeuvre.The dazzling extent and variety of Pearigen's work (i) __________ those who search for artistic continuity; nonetheless, certain ingrained values are (ii) __________ throughout his oeuvre.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　confound; reflected
reflect
reflect: standard unit headword
#263 SE
Although many aspects of the hippie movement of the 1960s seemed like superficial posturing, many hippies were not without a great deal of __________.Although many aspects of the hippie movement of the 1960s seemed like superficial posturing, many hippies were not without a great deal of __________.
答案：A, D　sincerity; earnestness
posture
posture: standard unit headword
#264 TC SINGLE

答案：C　capricious
prescientrather
prescient: standard unit headword | rather: standard unit headword

Based on the evidence available, it would be (i) __________ to insist on a wholly negative portrayal of King Prajadhipok. In his own writings and pronouncements as well as in firsthand accounts offered by others, Prajadhipok consistently emerges as (ii) __________ and even-tempered, though (iii) __________.Based on the evidence available, it would be (i) __________ to insist on a wholly negative portrayal of King Prajadhipok. In his own writings and pronouncements as well as in firsthand accounts offered by others, Prajadhipok consistently emerges as (ii) __________ and even-tempered, though (iii) __________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　facile; thoughtful; unexceptional

account: standard unit headword
#266 SE
The author's comparisons of Sontag's work to that of Walter Benjamin are __________: while the former is admittedly an influential figure, the latter is undoubtedly one of the most important critics of his era.The author's comparisons of Sontag's work to that of Walter Benjamin are __________: while the former is admittedly an influential figure, the latter is undoubtedly one of the most important critics of his era.
答案：A, B　overblown; inflated
inflate
inflate: standard unit headword
#267 TC DOUBLE
Intuitively, something is considered to be matter if, and only if, there is mass along with energy and/or momentum associated with it. In other words, possession of mass along with energy and/or momentum is regarded as (i) __________, rather than (ii) __________, property of matter.Intuitively, something is considered to be matter if, and only if, there is mass along with energy and/or momentum associated with it. In other words, possession of mass along with energy and/or momentum is regarded as (i) __________, rather than (ii) __________, property of matter.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　an essential; an accidental
rather
rather: standard unit headword
#268 TC TRIPLE
(i) __________, we can safely infer causality by appealing to a set of general principles (Newtonian mechanics, for example) that are well understood and have been shown to apply in comparably straightforward circumstances. However, for the opposing class, which consists of (ii) __________ systems like the global economy, human physiology, or the human mind itself, explanations of singular events are usually (iii) __________, absent the requisite accounting for the intricate dynamics of such systems.(i) __________, we can safely infer causality by appealing to a set of general principles (Newtonian mechanics, for example) that are well understood and have been shown to apply in comparably straightforward circumstances. However, for the opposing class, which consists of (ii) __________ systems like the global economy, human physiology, or the human mind itself, explanations of singular events are usually (iii) __________, absent the requisite accounting for the intricate dynamics of such systems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　In simple situations; complex; unjustified
account
account: standard unit headword
#269 SE
Although penguins have a long history on the African continent, Africa has at times become __________ individual penguin species, with several species being driven to extinction before the Middle Pleistocene.Although penguins have a long history on the African continent, Africa has at times become __________ individual penguin species, with several species being driven to extinction before the Middle Pleistocene.
答案：B, E　untenable for; uninhabitable for
untenable
untenable: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　descriptive; a normative
rather
rather: standard unit headword

Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, I　hypostatize; did not posit; regarded it as a process

rather: standard unit headword
#272 TC DOUBLE
Although people sometimes conflate them, the Beat poetry scene and the folk-music revival were actually (i) __________ and in many ways (ii) __________, despite sharing certain ancestral connections in Depression-era left-wing politics.Although people sometimes conflate them, the Beat poetry scene and the folk-music revival were actually (i) __________ and in many ways (ii) __________, despite sharing certain ancestral connections in Depression-era left-wing politics.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　distinct; antagonistic
antagonistic
antagonistic: standard unit headword

In the midst of so many trenchant articles, anything as __________ as this essay is bound to stand out as an anomaly.In the midst of so many trenchant articles, anything as __________ as this essay is bound to stand out as an anomaly.
答案：A, B　vapid; bland
bound
bound: standard unit headword
#274 TC TRIPLE
Rocke's history of the emergence of the theory of chemical structure encapsulates over 30 years of research on nineteenth-century chemistry. This is (i)__________, since structure theory emerged gradually through the efforts of a network of chemists distributed across several countries. Rocke focuses on just a few key figures, a strategy that necessarily involves some (ii)__________ of detail but that leads to (iii)__________ account of the subject.Rocke's history of the emergence of the theory of chemical structure encapsulates over 30 years of research on nineteenth-century chemistry. This is (i)__________, since structure theory emerged gradually through the efforts of a network of chemists distributed across several countries. Rocke focuses on just a few key figures, a strategy that necessarily involves some (ii)__________ of detail but that leads to (iii)__________ account of the subject.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　no mean feat; sacrifice; a digestible
account
account: standard unit headword
#275 TC DOUBLE
Throughout his career he has never displayed a disinterested respect for the truth--for what was actually said and done rather than for what the wishes had been. Given such a (i)__________ historical fact, it will certainly be seen as (ii)__________ that he should now embark on publishing a magazine ostensibly dedicated to history.Throughout his career he has never displayed a disinterested respect for the truth--for what was actually said and done rather than for what the wishes had been. Given such a (i)__________ historical fact, it will certainly be seen as (ii)__________ that he should now embark on publishing a magazine ostensibly dedicated to history.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　cavalier disregard for; ironic
rather
rather: standard unit headword

Critics of compost production often do not fully consider the potential environmental benefits of compost use - benefits that might __________ the energy consumption, gaseous emissions, and other negative environmental effects attributable to compost production.Critics of compost production often do not fully consider the potential environmental benefits of compost use - benefits that might __________ the energy consumption, gaseous emissions, and other negative environmental effects attributable to compost production.
答案：B, F　offset; counterbalance

Amelia Earhart, Freya Stark, and Gertrude Bell may be (ii)__________, but only a specialist would recognize the names of Fanny Workman or Janet Wulsin. Whether that is a matter of (iii)__________ or a fair reflection of their achievements is debatable.It is a common complaint that female explorers have been unfairly (i)__________ in modern historical accounts. Amelia Earhart, Freya Stark, and Gertrude Bell may be (ii)__________, but only a specialist would recognize the names of Fanny Workman or Janet Wulsin. Whether that is a matter of (iii)__________ or a fair reflection of their achievements is debatable.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　ignored; well enough known; prejudice

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　coarse; a subtle

The (i)__________ in local coverage of a rabies outbreak in colonial Hanoi may be startling today, but it cannot be attributed solely to the (ii)__________ of the era's journalists. Rather, before the advent of modern public health systems, (iii)__________ the unpleasantness of the disease and its treatment was seen as a way to ensure that the public remained vigilant against possible sources of contagion.The (i)__________ in local coverage of a rabies outbreak in colonial Hanoi may be startling today, but it cannot be attributed solely to the (ii)__________ of the era's journalists. Rather, before the advent of modern public health systems, (iii)__________ the unpleasantness of the disease and its treatment was seen as a way to ensure that the public remained vigilant against possible sources of contagion.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, G　degree of gruesome detail; sensationalism; underscoring

Each of these circumstances causes older bees to become cognitively (ii) __________, while juvenile bees, by contrast, exhibit a faster age-related cognitive decline.

Blank 2
答案：B, E　reversed; younger

Blank 1
Blank 2

Unfortunately, science publishers simply do not have space for detailed descriptions of experiments that fail along with discussions of every false start and blind alley, elements that are associated with even quite (iii) __________ endeavors.When a new scientific discovery is discussed in a scientific paper or book, the process of discovery is often represented as something (i) __________ and almost (ii) __________. Unfortunately, science publishers simply do not have space for detailed descriptions of experiments that fail along with discussions of every false start and blind alley, elements that are associated with even quite (iii) __________ endeavors.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　straightforward; inevitable; successful

Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) __________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) __________ as time passes.The levels of risk associated with construction and development in growing cities are (i) __________ as a consequence of urbanization, precautionary works, and environmental change. Because most risk assessment studies are the result of specific research grants or single contracts, they are seldom updated to reflect changes. Therefore, there is a danger that existing results on hazard and risk, although (ii) __________ at the time they were produced, could become (iii) __________ as time passes.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　dynamic; sound; misleading

Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) __________ rather than (ii) __________. Unfortunately, discussion of such solutions is marked by (i) __________ rather than (ii) __________. Blank 2

答案：C, D, G　exaggerated expectations; realism; preclude

答案：B, E　virtuosity; mastery

Both nostalgia and celebrity are ways for the observer to (i) __________ things that are exotic, and that are characterized by their (ii) __________ the ordinary, and to make them, instead, familiar matters upon which the observer's own feelings can feed.

答案：C, D　appropriate; difference from

答案：A, F　consequence; result

(ii) __________ by the (iii) __________ of the volunteers, Galaxy Zoo team was inspired to pursue lines of research they had never even imagined.

Blank 2
